serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of Tagliata di Tonno is approximately 300 g, comprising a seared yellowfin tuna loin portion with cherry tomatoes, black olives, rocket leaves, red onion, mozzarella, and a drizzle of extra virgin olive oil.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Yellowfin Tuna (cooked, dry heat) FDC ID 175159 and composite ingredient estimates for mozzarella, cherry tomatoes, black olives, arugula, red onion, and extra virgin olive oil
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Tagliata di Tonno is a composite restaurant dish not directly listed in any approved primary source. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Foundation Foods data for yellowfin tuna cooked dry heat (FDC ID 175159), part-skim mozzarella (FDC ID 173420), cherry tomatoes (FDC ID 170457), black olives (FDC ID 169094), arugula/rocket (FDC ID 170387), red onion (FDC ID 170000), and extra virgin olive oil (FDC ID 171413), weighted by approximate ingredient proportions typical of this dish. The input per_100g values from the grabfood record (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.7) were cross-checked against this USDA-based composite estimate and found to be broadly consistent. The USDA composite estimate is used as the authoritative basis. The input source URL https://foodo.sg/ does not correspond to the HPB Nutrient Composition of Food database and was not used.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — analogous estimate using mixed salad with protein and low-carbohydrate vegetables (GI approximately 32)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Tagliata di Tonno in the University of Sydney GI Database. An analogous estimate of GI 32 is applied based on the closest defensible comparator: a mixed protein-dominant salad with low-carbohydrate vegetables (cherry tomatoes, rocket, red onion) and dairy (mozzarella), consistent with GI values reported for mixed protein-vegetable dishes in Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281-2283). The dish contains only 4.0 g carbohydrate per 100 g; the dominant carbohydrate sources are cherry tomatoes (GI ~15) and mozzarella (GI ~27), both low-GI foods. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI 32 × available carbohydrate 12.0 g per 300 g serving / 100 = 3.84, rounded to 3.8 — classified as low GL. This estimation is conservative and clinically appropriate.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content at 217 mg per 100 g (651 mg per 300 g serving) from black olives, mozzarella, and seasoning
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be aware that a full 300 g serving delivers approximately 651 mg sodium — about 28% of the 2,300 mg daily limit — and should request reduced olives and mozzarella or a smaller portion; the omega-3 fatty acids from yellowfin tuna and oleocanthal from extra virgin olive oil provide beneficial vasodilatory and anti-inflammatory effects that partially offset sodium-related cardiovascular risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate content (4.0 g per 100 g) and high protein (15.0 g per 100 g) from yellowfin tuna
type2_diabetes.guidance: Tagliata di Tonno is well-suited for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its very low carbohydrate load (12 g per 300 g serving), low glycemic load of 3.8 per serving, high lean protein from yellowfin tuna which moderates postprandial glucose response, and healthy unsaturated fats from extra virgin olive oil; individuals should avoid adding bread or starchy sides to keep the total meal glycemic load low.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High omega-3 fatty acids from yellowfin tuna and oleocanthal polyphenols from extra virgin olive oil with low saturated fat (2.3 g per 100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Yellowfin tuna is a rich source of long-chain omega-3 fatty acids (EPA and DHA) which reduce triglycerides and improve endothelial function; combined with the anti-inflammatory oleocanthal in extra virgin olive oil and low saturated fat content, this dish is cardioprotective and consistent with Mediterranean dietary patterns recommended for cardiovascular disease prevention, though sodium from olives and mozzarella should be monitored in patients with concurrent hypertension.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Cherry tomatoes are a nightshade vegetable that may trigger solanine-related joint inflammation in RA-sensitive individuals
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis who are sensitive to nightshade vegetables should request omission of cherry tomatoes; the remaining components — yellowfin tuna omega-3s, extra virgin olive oil oleocanthal, and rocket leaf antioxidants — are strongly anti-inflammatory and beneficial for RA management.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Tagliata di Tonno is a classic Italian seared tuna dish, here presented as a restaurant-style plate featuring a black pepper crusted yellowfin tuna loin that is seared to medium-rare, then sliced and served over a bed of fresh rocket leaves (arugula) with cherry tomatoes, black olives, thinly sliced red onion, fresh mozzarella cheese, and Mediterranean herbs, finished with a generous drizzle of organic extra virgin olive oil. The dish originates from Italian coastal cuisine and is a seafood adaptation of the classic Tagliata di Manzo (sliced beef). The tuna is typically seared at high heat to form a flavourful pepper crust while retaining a pink, tender interior. The flavour profile is bold and savoury — the peppery crust contrasts with the mild, meaty tuna, the peppery bite of rocket, the briny olives, the sweet acidity of cherry tomatoes, and the creamy mozzarella, all unified by the fruity richness of extra virgin olive oil. Nutritionally, this dish is high in lean protein (15 g per 100 g), moderate in fat (8.3 g per 100 g, predominantly unsaturated from tuna and olive oil), very low in carbohydrates (4.0 g per 100 g), and provides meaningful calcium (50 mg per 100 g) from mozzarella and vitamin C (8.3 mg per 100 g) from cherry tomatoes and rocket. It is naturally gluten-free. Clinically, it is well-suited for Type 2 Diabetes management due to its very low glycemic load of 3.8 per 300 g serving, and supports cardiovascular health through omega-3 fatty acids from yellowfin tuna and polyphenols from extra virgin olive oil. Sodium is moderate at 217 mg per 100 g, primarily from black olives and mozzarella, and should be monitored by individuals with hypertension. A standard restaurant serving is approximately 300 g.

confidence_reason: Tagliata di Tonno is a composite restaurant dish not directly listed in any approved primary nutrient database. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central data for individual ingredients (yellowfin tuna cooked dry heat FDC ID 175159, mozzarella, cherry tomatoes, black olives, arugula, red onion, extra virgin olive oil) weighted by typical ingredient proportions. The input ai_generated values (confidence 0.7) were cross-checked against this USDA composite estimate and found broadly consistent. Confidence is rated medium because exact restaurant preparation proportions and seasoning quantities are not independently verified.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are USDA FoodData Central composite estimates for this multi-ingredient dish; no single approved source entry exists for Tagliata di Tonno as a prepared dish. Input grabfood/ai_generated values were used as a cross-check reference only and are not the authoritative basis. The input source URL https://foodo.sg/ does not correspond to the HPB Nutrient Composition of Food database and was not used as a nutrient source. 2. The following per_100g fields are set to -1 (data not available): monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g. Reason: precise composite values for this specific restaurant preparation are not reliably derivable from USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB database for the full dish as served; individual ingredient data exists in USDA FoodData Central but proportional weighting for this multi-component dish introduces uncertainty beyond acceptable precision for these micronutrients, and no single approved source reports these values for the composite dish. Source consulted: USDA FoodData Central (https://fdc.nal.usda.gov/, accessed 2024-01-01). 3. Glycemic index is set to 32 (low) as an analogous estimate based on the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281-2283) for mixed protein-dominant salad with low-carbohydrate vegetables; the dish contains only 4.0 g carbohydrate per 100 g. Glycemic load per 300 g serving = 3.8 (low). 4. The input ethnic_tags included 'Pasta', 'Pizza', and 'Chicken Dish' which are clearly erroneous for this dish and have been corrected. 5. trans_fat_g is reported as 0.03 g per 100 g, consistent with trace levels from mozzarella dairy fat; this is a naturally occurring trace amount, not an industrial trans fat. 6. Halal status is not confirmed as the dish contains mozzarella and the tuna preparation method (black pepper crust, searing) may involve non-halal additives depending on the restaurant; flagged as not confirmed.
